The Quick Launch Part 1
!
Now I want to talk about the quick launch.
!
The quick launch is a great way to bring in money fast, hence the name quick launch.
!
The caveat is that you do need to have some type of product and an offer, and you do
need to have a list. This isn’t for people who are just starting out. If you’re just starting
out, then you want to go with a seed launch.
!
If you have that offer and the list, it can be a very quick way to bring in some
momentum and money into your business. Also, to get people to jump into that buyer
column, to move them from the prospect list to the buyer list.
!
What you need is a story and some type of a special offer.
!
The special offer doesn’t have to be complicated. It could be some type of discount. It
could be an extra bonus. It could be an extra teleseminar. It doesn’t have to be fancy.
One very simple one, if you’re sitting there trying to rack your brain and decide what it
is, 50% off. Your offer can be great and give you a great quick launch, if you can swing
it, if you have that margin where you can give a 50% off discount.
!
If you have great backend offer so that after they purchase you’re going to have some
type of an up sell for them, either immediately or down the road, that can make sense.
If you can do 50% off, that’s great. It doesn’t have to be 50%. I’ve done many quick
launches that were 20% off and did really well.
!
You need some type of special offer. You can also just bundle stuff together, bundles
work really well. Take your main product and bundle in something else as a bonus or
put them together into a super bundle. That works great for a quick launch offer.
!
Next, you need that story. The story doesn’t have to be amazing. The classic example
that I’ve taught and I’ve been teaching for years, and I’m going to keep on teaching it
for years because it works really well. It worked well for me and it works for my Product
Launch Formula owners. That’s my tax sale. I’ll be teaching that in one of my upcoming
videos.
!
The story and basic premise behind this was that I got a big unexpected tax bill. My
business did really well, I took my taxes to the accountant and he gave them back to
me. He told me the numbers over the phone and he’s reading them off one digit at a
time, and then he read an extra digit, so it was not a happy day.
!
I was thinking, “Oh my gosh, what am I going to do?” Frankly, I had the money to pay
for it, but I didn’t want to write this huge check. So I put together a special offer and
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 4 --

did this super quick launch promotion. At the end of the day this is just a promotion, it’s
almost like a sale. And it worked really well, so that was the birth of the quick launch.
The funny thing about that is that I made a lot of money with my quick launch and then
teaching it to Product Launch Formula owners over the years, if we added up all of the
money that that tax bill has generated for all of the PLF owners, it’s hundreds of
thousands of dollars. It’s awesome and quick launches are very cool.
!
Now, your story doesn’t have to be about a tax bill. It can be about any number of
things. It doesn’t have to be all that amazing. It can be about special events like
weddings or birthdays, graduations, or you got a new office or a new home. It could be
about other financial calamities. I had a tax sale, you could have your basement flooded
or your pipes broke, or whatever. It could be something like, “I need to raise the
money.” That was the story of my tax sale, I just went out and told people, “I need to
raise money, that’s why I’m discounting this.”
!
The other thing you could do is it just be a simple customer appreciation or subscriber
appreciation, or reader appreciation. You go out and you say, “Thanks for being one of
my readers. I really appreciate you. Because of that, I’ve put together this amazing
special deal.” You do have to have a start and an end to the launch, like you do with
every launch. That’s what gets people moving.
!
You have the special offer, you have a reason for the special offer, you have a start and
an end, and you promote it, you mail people. Generally a quick launch is all done via
email. That makes it super simple.
!
One thing about quick launches is you’re not going to do a million dollars with one of
these, it’s just not the nature of them. But it is a really nice cash infusion and it moves
people from that prospect list over to your buyer list. If you remember, back when I was
talking about lists, there’s different types of lists. Two of them are the prospect list and
the buyers list. If you can get someone to buy something from you and move them into
that buyer column, onto that buyer list, they are worth 15 times more to you.
!
That number varies from market to market and business to business, but I’ve seen that
over and over in my business, it’s between 15 and 20 times more valuable to have
someone on that buyer list. If you have 1,000 people on your buyer list and 1,000
people on your prospect list, and you send each of them a promotion, that buyer list is
going to generate 15 times more dollars in sales.
!
If nothing else during a quick launch, moving people over into that buyer list is super
powerful. It’s also a really great way to bring in money. Quick launches are very cool
because they’re simple, they’re easy, they’re generally just email, and all you have to
put together that special offer and have that story behind it, have a start and an end to
the launch, and you win.
!
I remember the first time I went furniture shopping and we were so just desperately
poor. When we were newly married and our kids were little, I can remember vividly
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module3v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 4 --

when I went furniture shopping for the very first time. It wasn’t that long ago – before
that, it was all hand-me-downs. In any case, we went to this one furniture store and
they were having this big sale throughout the whole store. Everything in the store was
on sale, they had this sale ticket. I thought, “This is really amazing.”
!
Since it was the first time we bought furniture, my wife and I were not ready to make
these big decisions. It was a big decision. So we held off and the sale ended on
Tuesday, and we didn’t buy anything.
!
We wanted a couple weeks and we went back to that same store and they were having
another sale, a different sale. Everything in the entire store was on sale. I thought,
“That’s sort of weird.” Because we take so long to make decisions about purchases like
that, we held off again.
!
We went back two weeks later and they were having another sale and everything was
marked off. We quickly realized that with this store everything was always on sale.
In my opinion, you don’t want a business where everything is always on sale or where
you’re frequently having sales every month. Then people are going to be looking at
your business saying, “I’m interested in buying this, but I’m just going to wait a little
while, because I know it’s going to go on sale.” I don’t ever want to have that kind of
positioning.
!
Remember, we talked about positioning earlier. You don’t want to ever have the kind of
positioning where people just expect discounts from you and they’re just going to wait
for a sale. You don’t want to do continually do these quick launches. That is also the
reason why you want to go out and have a story and a reason behind the discount. You
want there to be a good solid reason for it that justifies that special deal that you’re
giving people. That’s just something to think about with your quick launches.
!
They’re simple, they’re easy, they’re actually fun. They’re lucrative and they move
people from being prospects to being buyers. With that said, we’re going to get to some
examples now.
